{"key_event":"Postwar consensus and rollback of wartime controls","reasoning":"The state remained exceptionally capable through the welfare settlement, taxation, NATO/rearmament, nuclear weapons, and expanding regulatory functions, but the end of rationing, identity cards, some controls, partial denationalization, and Suez-imperial limits pulled it down from wartime near-maximum penetration. Civil society stayed dominant and edged higher as full-employment trade unionism, social rights, consumer autonomy, anti-nuclear mobilization, and a more adaptable Parliament strengthened organized checks, despite security secrecy and racial tensions.","society_change":0.1,"society_power":9.8,"state_change":-0.3,"state_power":9.6}
